description: 39 - 31-12-2024 - Emails between Peter Mandelson and Robert Tinline (Americas irector, FCDO)

participants.name: Emails between Peter Mandelson and Robert Tinline

text:
irector, FCDO)

From: Peter Mandelson Personal

Sent: Tue 31/12/2024 5:42:24 PM (UTC)

To: Robert Tinline Personal lain
Griffiths[ Personal

Subject: Re: Chronological CV

AND HAPPY NEW YEAR TO YOU BOTH
From: Robert Tinline

Date: Tuesday, 31 December 2024 at 17:41
To: Peter Mandelson , lain Griffiths
Subject: RE: Chronological CV

OFFICIAL

We can certainly look at how we construct a programme — some of the more mundane

domestic things and unclassified externals at the start. We've warmed people up so should be

able to put get a programme pulled together swiftly once we have our marching orders. We'll

see what we get back from the Foreign Sec's team on Thursday am. Happy new year ..Rob

Rob Tinline | Americas Director | Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office
Personal

OFFICIAL

From: Peter Mandelson

Sent: 31 December 2024 17:31

To: Robert Tinline ; lain Griffiths

Subject: Re: Chronological CV

| don't think we should overthink the speaking engagement. It's private panel not public, for half
an hour and very general.

How long does the security clearance take ? Would it be better to frontload the non classified
calls eg with ministers and some of the people | want to see like Simon Fraser, Peter Hill, ex
HMAs etc ?

Peter

From: Robert Tinline < Personal

Date: Tuesday, 31 December 2024 at 17:20

To: Peter Mandelson Personal , lain Griffiths

Personal
Subject: RE: Chronological CV

OFFICIAL

There'll be a ceiling on classification without clearance, but | don't think that's the primary issue.
The main question we're trying to clarify is around impact of the speaking engagement and the
delay to start date —_ if the logic for that is that you remain a private individual at the point of the
speaking engagement, how far is that consistent with beginning a full briefing round in advance
of it? Given the potential for him to have to explain our approach, the Foreign Secretary's team
need to tell us how they want to proceed.

Rob

Rob Tinline | Americas Director | Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

123

OFFICIAL
From: Peter Mandelson 
 
Sent: 31 December 2024 17:03
To: Robert Tinline 
>; Iain Griffiths 
 
Subject: Re: Chronological CV
Edward and Gordon will get along famously.
I would much prefer to get going with calls etc w/b Jan 6 but will policy briefings/reading 
depend on vetting clearance ?
From: Robert Tinline 
Date: Tuesday, 31 December 2024 at 16:47
To: Peter Mandelson 
>, Iain Griffiths 
Subject: RE: Chronological CV
OFFICIAL
Latest I had from Philip/
 was we were working to Monday 13 January start. Obviously all 
the vetting, medical etc needs to proceed regardless, but we are checking what on 13 (or 14) 
January start date means for the week of 6 January - whether we’ re to set up policy briefings / 
official meetings during that week or wait for your contractual start. We’ ve started contacting 
offices, but not fixed dates/times yet. We’ ll come back to that on Thursday.
Assume you’ re aware it’ s looking like Duke of Edinburgh and Gordon Brown going to the 
Carter funeral for the UK.
Rob
Rob Tinline | Americas Director | Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office 
OFFICIAL
From: Peter Mandelson 
 
Sent: 31 December 2024 16:06
To: Iain Griffiths 
Cc: Robert Tinline 
Subject: Re: Chronological CV
Don’t worry, I called Philip and bored him instead.
Is my start date on January 14 ?
Peter
Sent from my iPhone
On 31 Dec 2024, at 15:05, Iain Griffiths 
> 
wrote:
OFFICIAL
Hi Peter,
I'm overseas this week - Rob will be available for a chat though. If you 
can't reach him let me know?
Iain
Iain Griffiths
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
124


From: Peter Mandelson Personal
Sent: Tuesday, December 31, 2024 5:49 am
To: lain Griffiths Personal

Subject: Re: Chronological CV

Are you away or around for chat ?

Sent from my iPhone

On 27 Dec 2024, at 13:28, lain Griffiths
Personal wrote:

OFFICIAL

Dear Peter,

As per our WhatsApp exchange, please find attached a draft version
of your CV in chronological format, as requested by State
Department. This draft runs throughout your previous time in
government, but we have not added in your private sector work since
2010.

JCS (PS/HMA in DC - cc'ed) has pulled this together and,
subject to your comments/additions, can submit it to State early next
week.

Best,

lain

Iain Griffiths (he/him) | Head of US & Canada Department | Foreign,
Commonwealth & Development Office |

Email: Personal

125
parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
